desc: Ohrid is a lakeside UNESCO destination combining Orthodox heritage sites, summer swimming, and relaxed old-town evenings on the Balkan overland corridor.
hls: UNESCO old town and lake churches
Easy links to Albania and Skopje routes

todo.name: Old town church circuit
todo.desc: Walk St. Sophia, Plaošnik, and Kaneo viewpoints.

todo.name: Lake Ohrid boat cruise
todo.desc: Take a half-day cruise to lakeside monasteries and coves.

todo.name: Samuel's Fortress sunrise
todo.desc: Climb early for panoramic lake and old-town views.

todo.name: Trpejca or Ljubaništa beach day
todo.desc: Use a day for swimming in clearer southern lake waters.

todo.name: Bay of Bones museum stop
todo.desc: Visit reconstructed stilt settlement exhibits on the southeast shore.

todo.name: Čaršija dinner crawl
todo.desc: Sample grilled trout and tavče gravče in old bazaar lanes.

wifi.note: Generally solid in old-town hotels; speed dips at full summer occupancy.

prac.fltNote: Seasonal nonstop UK routes appear in summer to Ohrid/Skopje, while shoulder and winter travel often requires one-stop Balkan or Central Europe connections.

airports.name: Ohrid St. Paul the Apostle Airport

prac.currency: North Macedonia local currency (check live rates before booking).
prac.lang: Local language plus tourism-level English in main visitor areas.
prac.tz: Check local timezone and DST rules for exact travel dates.
prac.visa: Confirm current entry/transit rules for your passport before booking.
alerts: Lakefront roads and parking are congested in July-August evenings.
Some beach clubs and boat operators are cash-only despite card terminals in town restaurants.
Winter service frequency drops sharply for intercity buses—check last departures in advance.

sweet: May-Jun and Sep for warm lake weather with lighter crowds than July-August peaks.
